domain: Senegalese sabar drum tradition (Wolof/Lebou/Serere): sabar ensemble + lead drummer; tama talking-drum lead/teasing drum that converses with vocalist; bakks (pre-composed classical sabar phrases, not improvised) form fixed dialect-utterances; drummers improvise on top of bakks structure; conversation polyrhythm (one player asks 'I'm hungry' + another answers 'me too!' via rhythmic patterns); Wolof not tonal but rhythms follow linguistic patterns; sabar at naming ceremonies + weddings + rituals
specific_mechanism: SABAR-BAKKS-TAMA-LEAD-CONVERSATION: K-agent polyrhythmic conversation + bakks fixed-utterance dictionary + tama lead-query + linguistic-pattern constraint + ask-respond bind. (1) K_bakks fixed-utterance dictionary: pre-defined K canonical agent-message-types (bakks classical sabar phrases); each agent's outputs drawn from this fixed bag of utterance-types. (2) Tama lead-agent: 1 designated tama LLM that initiates each conversational round + queries/teases other agents (lead drummer role). (3) Polyrhythmic K-agent concurrent communication: K agents communicate without strict round-robin; each agent at own response-frequency f_k (own rhythm); interleaved emit-respond cycle. (4) Wolof linguistic-pattern non-tonal constraint: response from each agent must follow linguistic-pattern P_lex (Wolof-like non-tonal rhythmic-pattern matching) — pattern-template-match required for valid response. (5) Ask-respond bind: each ask-type bakks utterance triggers ≥1 matching respond-type bakks utterance from another agent (bind constraint enforces dialogic coherence). (6) Differs from R488 MAQAM-LEAD-TARJAMAH-DIALOGUE (solo+ensemble + modal-anchor + tarjamah-paraphrase, no bakks dictionary + no polyrhythmic concurrent) and R500 QULLIQ-HEARTH-FLAMEKEEPER (flame-keeper-anchor + voice-rotation ordered, no bakks fixed + no polyrhythmic) and R513 UMUNNA-KINDRED-SPOKESPERSON-2-TIER (2-tier cluster + spokesperson, no bakks + no concurrent polyrhythm + no ask-respond bind) by bakks fixed-utterance dictionary + tama-lead query + polyrhythmic concurrent K-agent + linguistic-pattern non-tonal constraint + ask-respond bind.
llm_application: SABAR-BAKKS-TAMA-LEAD-CONVERSATION K_bakks fixed-utterance pre-defined dictionary + tama lead-LLM query-tease initiating + K-agent polyrhythmic concurrent communication each own response-frequency f_k + Wolof linguistic-pattern P_lex non-tonal response-constraint + ask-respond bind enforcing matching respond-type per ask-type. Differs from R392 MAASAI. R403 WELSH-PENILLION. R411 IROQUOIS. R443 ALUNA. R449 MAPUCHE. R463 HOKULEA. R465 SALGAN. R488 MAQAM-LEAD-TARJAMAH (closest — lead+ensemble, no bakks dictionary). R500 QULLIQ-HEARTH-FLAMEKEEPER (closest — flame-keeper anchor, no bakks + no concurrent). R513 UMUNNA-KINDRED-SPOKESPERSON (closest — 2-tier, no bakks + no concurrent + no ask-respond). Differs from Multi-Agent Protocol Survey + AgentsNet + Emergent Coordination + Which Protocol Choose by bakks fixed dictionary + tama lead-query + polyrhythmic concurrent + linguistic-pattern + ask-respond bind. Differs by BAKKS FIXED DICTIONARY + TAMA LEAD + POLYRHYTHMIC CONCURRENT + LINGUISTIC PATTERN + ASK-RESPOND BIND.
novelty_claim: Multi-Agent Protocol Survey + AgentsNet + Emergent Coordination + Which Protocol literature cover multi-agent communication + protocol + coordination space. SABAR-BAKKS-TAMA-LEAD-CONVERSATION adds (a) K_bakks fixed-utterance dictionary + (b) tama lead-agent query-tease + (c) polyrhythmic K-agent concurrent each own-frequency + (d) Wolof linguistic-pattern non-tonal constraint + (e) ask-respond bind. Recombination — borderline lookup.
